basics.name: Joe Pegler
basics.label: Founding Engineer | Web3 Infrastructure | Account Abstraction (ERC-4337)
basics.summary: Founding-leaning infrastructure engineer building transaction systems end to end: bundlers, SDKs, and execution pipelines. Own ERC-4337 account abstraction and cross-chain infra across EVM and Solana. Operate in 0 to 1 environments with high ambiguity and hard reliability constraints.

selectedImpact: Shipped an in-house ERC-4337 bundler with retry logic, EntryPoint v0.7 simulation, and structured decoding, enabling stable gasless and sponsored user-op flows.
Owned the TypeScript smart-account SDK at Biconomy, including EIP-712/1271/7739/6492 signing and P-256 passkeys, to unblock production account abstraction integrations.
Built defi.app execution infrastructure: multi-chain quote and route orchestration, DeFi action providers, and EVM plus Solana simulation and paymaster systems.
Led multiple 0 to 1 product surfaces, from dApp and intents execution at Enso to automated CI/CD for business-critical trading interfaces at CoinFLEX.

work.name: defi.app
work.summary: Own transaction infrastructure and developer tooling for a unified cross-chain DeFi product.
highlights: Designed and shipped an internal ERC-4337 bundler with retries, EntryPoint v0.7 simulation, and Sourcify plus ABI-backed error decoding to increase user-op delivery reliability.
Built cross-chain quote and route orchestration across LiFi, Jupiter, 1inch, Odos, Mayan, LayerZero, and Relay to expose one execution surface instead of chain-specific flows.
Owned the TypeScript SDK (RouteClient and ExecutionManager) for account abstraction, quote, and execution paths, reducing integration load for product teams.
Delivered EVM plus Solana simulation and paymaster infrastructure, including Jito-aware validation, enabling sponsored transactions across both ecosystems.

work.name: biconomy
work.summary: Owned the core developer SDK for ERC-4337 smart accounts and multi-chain execution.
highlights: Led architecture and API design for the flagship TypeScript SDK used by teams building ERC-4337 smart-account products.
Implemented EIP-712, EIP-1271, EIP-7739, and EIP-6492 signing with P-256 passkey support to enable keyless and gasless UX patterns.
Shipped viem-based modular clients with ERC-7579 decorators and Safe support, cutting complexity for account, paymaster, and bundler integration paths.
Hardened production readiness with typed errors, composable batching, EntryPoint v0.7 support, and CI coverage with Vitest, Bun, GitHub Actions, and Codecov.

work.name: enso
work.summary: Led 0 to 1 delivery of Enso's client dApp and execution stack.
highlights: Owned end-to-end delivery of the primary Ethereum dApp, from frontend experience to backend execution, while requirements and product scope were still moving.
Built and operated the NestJS backend and integration layer with retry and failure handling to keep execution reliable in production.
Shipped and extended intents plus solver infrastructure for decentralized trade execution, enabling intent-driven UX and competitive solver paths.

work.name: coinflex
work.summary: Owned the trading interface and deployment system for a business-critical exchange product.
highlights: Led delivery of the platform's trading UI, owning architecture, core feature rollout, and engineering standards across a small team.
Built fully automated CI/CD for the interface stack, reducing release risk and supporting faster iteration on a live trading product.

earlierExperience: GameAnalytics - Lead Frontend Developer (2016 to 2019): led a team of five, set stack and quality practices, awarded company MVP 2018/19.
FSI, Healthcare 21, and Nixatel (2013 to 2016): delivered cross-platform enterprise products and 0 to 1 mobile plus web systems in small teams.
